prompt: Tasks please for the logsql CLI tool. TDD is at /Users/nhoze/.claude/skills/tech-lead/evals/tdd-logsql-cli.md. Write them as a local directory, that's what the team uses for personal projects.

assertions.text: A tasks-* directory (e.g. tasks-logsql-cli/) was created inside outputs/.
The tasks directory contains a README.md index.
Total files inside tasks-*/ is between 5 and 13 (≤ 12 tasks + 1 README).
Task count is at least 4 (excluding README).
Every task file contains all six required fields: Title, One-liner, Composes, TDD sections addressed, Depends on, Acceptance criteria.
No task file contains code blocks, function signatures, pseudocode, or shell commands inside its body.
No task body references specific source file paths (e.g. 'cmd/logsql/main.go', 'pkg/parser/parser.go', 'src/' paths).
No task has 'decide on language' or 'choose Go vs Python' as a deliverable. The TDD already decided Go; tasks must respect that decision.
No task asks to decide parser strictness. The TDD already decided strict.
No task has 'write tests' or 'add test coverage' as its primary deliverable.
No task is a standalone 'set up the project skeleton' / 'init Go module' / 'create the package' task — scaffolding is folded into the first feature task.
The breakdown does NOT consist of horizontal layers ('implement parser', 'implement executor', 'implement output formatter' as 3 separate tasks). If it does, that's a major mindset failure.

The first task in dependency order is a walking skeleton — a minimal end-to-end CLI that reads JSON-lines from STDIN and emits output for the simplest SELECT * WHERE LIMIT case. NOT 'implement the parser' alone.
No task is a standalone 'address performance' / 'memory budget' / 'cold-start latency' line item. NFRs ride with feature tasks in their acceptance criteria.
The exit-code convention (including the deliberate exit 1 for zero-rows-matched) appears in the acceptance criteria of the task that introduces it.
The task that implements GROUP BY / ORDER BY (the blocking operators) has memory-budget enforcement and the named-runtime-error behavior in its acceptance criteria.
If the breakdown depends on the unresolved SQL-subset open question (§10.1), it is surfaced as a BLOCKER in the README/summary — not invented as a task.
The transcript contains an acknowledgement line confirming the TDD was read end-to-end.
The chat summary names the local directory destination and the task count.
